page_content:
ENGL291 Writing, Revising, Persuading (3 Credits) Intermediate-level, writing-intensive course for students who have successfully satisﬁed the Fundamental Studies Academic Writing requirement but wish to hone skills in analyzing and producing rhetorically attuned, well-styled prose. Deeper study of rhetorical theory and its application to a wide variety of arguments and situations. Additional writing practice, techniques of revision, study of effect of stylistic choices. Topics may include argumentation theory, visual rhetoric, stylistic theory, and writing theory. Prerequisite: Must have satisﬁed Fundamental Studies Academic Writing requirement.

ENGL292 Writing for Change (3 Credits) Service learning in collaboration with students at area high schools. Explores how writing can be a tool for social change. Participants serve as mentors, create a performance event concerning a pressing social issue, and compose reflections, literacy narratives, publicity materials, and a multimodal project. Focus on developing critical self-awareness. Prerequisite: Permission of ARHU-English department. Recommended: ENGL101. Jointly offered with: ENGL388C. Restriction: Requires application and references. Credit Only Granted for: ENGL292 or ENGL388C.
